Auction Terms & Conditions Agreement

Matex Materials Exchange Platform  |  B2B Industrial & Construction Materials

Auction ID {{AUCTION_ID}}
Auction Type {{AUCTION_TYPE}}
Opening Date & Time {{AUCTION_OPEN_DATETIME}}
Closing Date & Time {{AUCTION_CLOSE_DATETIME}}
Reserve Price {{RESERVE_PRICE_CAD}}
Bid Increment {{BID_INCREMENT_CAD}}
Buyer's Premium {{BUYERS_PREMIUM_PCT}}
Deposit Required {{DEPOSIT_REQUIRED_CAD}}
Governing Province {{GOVERNING_PROVINCE}}
Agreement Date {{AGREEMENT_DATE}}

2. Lot Description & Specifications
# Material / Item Unit Quantity Grade / Spec Location Est. Value (CAD)
1 {{LOT_1_DESCRIPTION}} {{LOT_1_UNIT}} {{LOT_1_QTY}} {{LOT_1_GRADE}} {{LOT_1_LOCATION}} {{LOT_1_VALUE}}
2 {{LOT_2_DESCRIPTION}} {{LOT_2_UNIT}} {{LOT_2_QTY}} {{LOT_2_GRADE}} {{LOT_2_LOCATION}} {{LOT_2_VALUE}}
Total Estimated Value (CAD) {{TOTAL_LOT_VALUE_CAD}}

Condition: {{LOT_CONDITION}}  |  Inspection Period: {{INSPECTION_WINDOW}}  |  Pickup/Delivery Deadline: {{DELIVERY_DEADLINE}}

3. Registration & Bidder Eligibility
  1. All bidders must hold a verified Matex account at a minimum KYC Level {{MIN_KYC_LEVEL}} prior to placing any bid.
  2. Bidders must be registered businesses or sole proprietors operating legally within Canada, or such other jurisdictions as Matex may approve in writing.
  3. A refundable participation deposit of {{DEPOSIT_REQUIRED_CAD}} (CAD) must be held in escrow on the Matex platform before the bidder is granted bidding access. The deposit is forfeited if the winning bidder defaults.
  4. Matex reserves the right to refuse, revoke, or disqualify any registration at its sole discretion without liability.
  5. Bidder registration constitutes unconditional acceptance of these Terms & Conditions.
4. Bidding Rules
  1. Opening Bid: The auction opens at a starting price of {{OPENING_BID_CAD}} (CAD).
  2. Bid Increments: Each subsequent bid must exceed the current highest bid by a minimum increment of {{BID_INCREMENT_CAD}} (CAD).
  3. Reserve Price: The lot carries a reserve price of {{RESERVE_PRICE_CAD}} (CAD). Matex will not be obligated to sell below this price. If the reserve is not met, the Seller may elect to sell to the highest bidder at their sole discretion.
  4. Bid Retractions: All bids are irrevocable and legally binding once submitted. Bid retraction is not permitted.
  5. Auto-Extend: If a bid is placed within the last {{ANTI_SNIPE_MINUTES}} minutes of the auction, the closing time automatically extends by {{ANTI_SNIPE_MINUTES}} minutes.
  6. Proxy Bidding: Matex {{PROXY_BIDDING_ALLOWED}} proxy/automatic bidding for this auction.
  7. Rejected Bids: Matex may reject any bid placed in error, placed in bad faith, or placed by a suspended user without notice.
5. Buyer's Premium & Platform Fees
  1. The winning bidder shall pay a Buyer's Premium of {{BUYERS_PREMIUM_PCT}} on the final hammer price, in addition to the hammer price itself.
  2. Seller's commission payable to Matex: {{SELLER_COMMISSION_PCT}} of the hammer price, deducted from sale proceeds.
  3. All amounts are in Canadian Dollars (CAD) and are exclusive of applicable taxes (HST/GST/QST). The winning bidder is responsible for all applicable taxes.
  4. Payment processing fees (if any) shall be disclosed at checkout and are the responsibility of the buyer.
6. Payment Terms
  1. Upon auction close, the winning bidder will receive an invoice for the total amount due: Hammer Price + Buyer's Premium + applicable taxes.
  2. Full payment must be received within {{PAYMENT_DEADLINE_DAYS}} business days of auction close.
  3. Accepted payment methods: Matex Escrow Wallet, Wire Transfer (EFT), or such other methods as Matex may approve. Cheques are not accepted.
  4. All funds are held in Matex's managed escrow account until (a) the buyer confirms receipt and acceptance of goods, or (b) {{ESCROW_AUTO_RELEASE_DAYS}} days have elapsed without a dispute being filed, whichever is earlier.
  5. Escrow funds will be released to the Seller upon successful delivery confirmation or expiry of the dispute window.
  6. Default: Failure to pay within the prescribed period constitutes default. The participation deposit will be forfeited, the sale will be cancelled, and Matex may offer the lot to the next-highest qualified bidder.
7. Inspection & Due Diligence
  1. Bidders are granted an inspection window of {{INSPECTION_WINDOW}} prior to auction close to inspect the lots at: {{INSPECTION_ADDRESS}}.
  2. Inspection appointments must be scheduled via the Matex platform no later than {{INSPECTION_BOOKING_DEADLINE}}.
  3. All lots are sold "as-is, where-is" unless a written condition warranty is provided by the Seller and attached as Schedule A to this Agreement.
  4. The Seller warrants that the description of the lots in Section 2 is materially accurate and that the Seller has clear title to all items offered.
  5. By placing a bid, the bidder acknowledges having conducted sufficient due diligence and waives the right to rescind based on condition unless fraud or material misrepresentation is proven.
8. Delivery, Logistics & Risk of Loss
  1. Delivery terms for this auction: {{INCOTERMS}} (e.g., EXW, DAP, FCA).
  2. Pickup/delivery must be completed by {{DELIVERY_DEADLINE}}. Storage fees of {{STORAGE_FEE_PER_DAY_CAD}} per day will accrue after this date.
  3. Risk of loss and title transfer to the Buyer upon: {{TITLE_TRANSFER_TRIGGER}} (e.g., pickup by buyer's carrier, delivery to buyer's address).
  4. The Buyer is responsible for arranging carrier insurance adequate to cover the value of the goods in transit.
  5. Matex logistics carriers (if selected) are booked through the platform. Carrier liability is governed by applicable carrier tariffs and the Canada Transportation Act.
9. Title, Warranties & Representations
  1. The Seller represents and warrants that: (a) it holds clear, unencumbered title to all lots offered; (b) no liens, charges, or security interests attach to the lots except as disclosed in Schedule A; (c) the Seller has full authority to sell the lots.
  2. Except as expressly stated in writing, no warranty of merchantability, fitness for purpose, or quality is given by either Matex or the Seller.
  3. Matex acts solely as platform intermediary and assumes no liability for the quality, condition, or title of any lot.
10. Dispute Resolution
  1. Any dispute arising from this auction must be filed through the Matex platform within {{DISPUTE_WINDOW_DAYS}} days of delivery or the scheduled delivery date, whichever is earlier.
  2. Matex's dispute resolution team will review submissions from both parties and issue a preliminary determination within {{DISPUTE_REVIEW_DAYS}} business days.
  3. If either party contests Matex's determination, the matter shall be referred to binding arbitration under the rules of the ADR Institute of Canada, with proceedings conducted in {{GOVERNING_PROVINCE}}.
  4. The language of arbitration proceedings shall be {{ARBITRATION_LANGUAGE}}.
  5. Pending resolution of any dispute, escrow funds shall remain frozen in the Matex platform.
11. Confidentiality
  1. The identity of the winning bidder and all financial terms of this transaction shall remain confidential and shall not be disclosed to third parties by either the Seller or the Buyer without the prior written consent of the other party or Matex, except as required by law.
  2. Bid history (without bidder identities) may be published on the Matex platform for transparency purposes.
12. Governing Law & Jurisdiction

This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the Province of {{GOVERNING_PROVINCE}} and the federal laws of Canada applicable therein. The parties irrevocably submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of {{GOVERNING_PROVINCE}}.

13. General Provisions
  1. Entire Agreement: This Agreement, together with any attached Schedules, constitutes the entire agreement between the parties with respect to the auction and supersedes all prior discussions.
  2. Amendments: No amendment to these terms is effective unless made in writing and signed by Matex.
  3. Severability: If any provision is found unenforceable, the remaining provisions continue in full force.
  4. Force Majeure: Neither party shall be liable for delays caused by circumstances beyond their reasonable control, including but not limited to natural disasters, strikes, or government orders.
  5. Electronic Execution: This Agreement may be executed electronically via Matex's e-signature module (powered by DocuSign / Matex eSign). Electronic signatures shall have the same legal effect as wet-ink signatures pursuant to applicable Canadian electronic commerce legislation.
⚠ IMPORTANT: By signing below, all parties confirm they have read, understood, and agree to be bound by these Terms & Conditions in their entirety.
